instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
llm_response:
The development team will commence the week with a collaborative brainstorming session to define the core requirements for the new app feature. Each member must review the initial design documents and submit a status update by the end of the first day. This structured approach ensures that all stakeholders are aligned before significant coding efforts begin.

The schedule allocates specific blocks for intensive coding, rigorous testing, and strategic planning without any dedicated meeting slots. Developers will focus on implementing the backend logic during the morning hours while QA engineers prepare test cases in the afternoon. A detailed guide on agile methodologies is available at [Agile Manifesto](https://agilemanifesto.org/) to support these activities. The team will conduct a final code review before deploying the feature to the staging environment. All participants are expected to maintain a high standard of quality throughout the week.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict: it asks to "Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings" but later states "Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule." The model ignores this conflict and provides a response that follows the "no meetings" constraint (while including a brainstorming session, which is a type of meeting, it treats it as a task rather than a scheduled meeting block). However, the model fails to follow several other formatting constraints: it does not use Markdown headers (it uses no headers at all), it does not use unordered lists, and it does not follow the specific sentence count per paragraph (the first paragraph has 3 sentences, but the second has 6 sentences instead of 5). Since the model did not acknowledge the conflict and simply provided a response, it falls under category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
